title: More Nonsense Limerick 7
content:
There was an old man of Dunrose;
    A parrot seized hold of his nose.
    When he grew melancholy, 
    They said, "His name's Polly,"
    Which soothed that old man of Dunrose.
meaning:
Edward Lear's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "More Nonsense Limerick 7"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
